executive_brief:
The threat landscape has undergone a structural fracture this week. AI is no longer a theoretical risk; it is the operational engine of offense. The University of Toronto's demonstration of an autonomous AI worm, combined with the live deployment of the 'Hades' malware family that actively deceives AI security scanners, proves that adaptive, self-replicating malware is now viable. This acceleration is forcing a crisis in vulnerability management. Microsoft released a record-breaking 206 patches in June's Patch Tuesday, yet the defense perimeter is crumbling. Researcher Nightmare Eclipse immediately deployed 'RoguePlanet' and 'GreatXML' exploits that bypass fully patched Windows 10/11 systems and break BitLocker encryption, demonstrating that the window between patch release and functional exploitation has collapsed to hours.

Simultaneously, the software supply chain is the epicenter of a new generation of wormable attacks. The 'Miasma' worm has compromised 73 Microsoft GitHub repositories, triggering a supply chain cascade that forced GitHub to disable automatic npm script execution by default—a fundamental shift in developer tooling security. Critical infrastructure is bleeding: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN and Check Point VPNs face actively exploited zero-days with no patches or delayed fixes, while Ivanti Sentry suffers maximum-severity remote code execution. CISA's emergency directive mandating 3-day patching for federal agencies confirms that the industry's traditional patching cadence is obsolete. Organizations must pivot from reactive severity-based patching to continuous attack simulation and assume that AI-assisted exploitation is happening in real-time.

threat_stories.headline: Miasma Worm Weaponizes GitHub/npm, Forcing Emergency Supply Chain Overhaul
threat_stories.narrative:
The 'Miasma' worm has evolved from a supply chain threat into a platform-level crisis. Operating under the alias TeamPCP, this worm targets the GitHub/npm ecosystem, compromising 73 Microsoft repositories and disrupting CI/CD pipelines globally. The worm leverages the automatic execution of npm install scripts to propagate, injecting malicious code into dependencies and spreading laterally across repositories. The attack is so severe that GitHub has been forced to disable automatic npm install script execution by default in npm v12, a change that fundamentally alters the behavior of the world's most popular package manager.

The sophistication of Miasma is compounded by the emergence of the 'Hades' malware family, which is actively poisoning Python and JavaScript packages. Hades embeds obfuscated scripts in package `__init__.py` files and uses the Bun runtime to execute payloads, bypassing Node.js dependencies and proxy logs. Crucially, Hades includes adversarial prompt injection techniques that lie to AI-based code scanners, causing LLM security tools to ignore the malicious code. Attackers are also using GitHub Actions, OIDC, and Sigstore to generate valid provenance bundles, allowing them to republish compromised packages with cryptographic signatures that appear legitimate.

This represents a paradigm shift: supply chain attacks are no longer just about compromising a single package; they are about automating the compromise of the trust model itself. The combination of wormable propagation, AI-evasion, and cryptographic forgery means that developers can no longer trust standard dependency scanning or package signatures without rigorous behavioral validation.

timeline.event: GitHub pulls 70+ Microsoft repositories offline amid suspected Miasma worm infections, breaking CI/CD pipelines.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates the operational impact of supply chain attacks on major technology vendors.

timeline.event: Miasma source code briefly leaks on GitHub, and IronWorm operators deploy new Miasma variants to npm.
timeline.significance: Leak accelerates adoption by other threat actors; variants increase attack surface.

timeline.event: GitHub announces npm v12 will disable automatic install script execution by default to block supply chain attacks.
timeline.significance: Forces a massive migration effort for developers and breaks backward compatibility for legitimate workflows.

timeline.event: Hades campaign details emerge, showing malware that evades AI scanners and uses Bun runtime to bypass detection.
timeline.significance: Proves attackers are actively weaponizing AI-evasion techniques in supply chain payloads.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Software development organizations, CI/CD pipelines, and any environment relying on npm/PyPI dependencies are at critical risk. The Miasma worm achieves repository takeover and credential theft. Hades leads to credential exfiltration, token theft, and potential wiper deployment. The npm default change will cause build failures for organizations not prepared to migrate to v12 or configure allowlists.
threat_stories.action_required: Immediately upgrade to npm v12 or configure `allow-scripts` to off in npm v11.10.0+. Audit all CI/CD pipelines for unauthorized script execution. Block the Bun runtime execution on build servers and developer workstations unless explicitly required. Implement strict allowlists for package installations and verify cryptographic signatures against known-good sources. Rotate all GitHub tokens and OIDC credentials exposed in compromised repositories.

threat_stories.headline: Nightmare Eclipse vs. Microsoft: Zero-Days Bypass Patched Windows and Break BitLocker
threat_stories.narrative:
A high-profile feud between security researcher Nightmare Eclipse and Microsoft has escalated into a real-world security emergency. During the record-breaking June Patch Tuesday, Microsoft addressed 206 vulnerabilities, including three zero-days. However, within hours, Nightmare Eclipse deployed 'RoguePlanet,' a race condition exploit targeting Microsoft Defender that grants SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows 10 and 11 systems. The exploit leverages path redirection and ISO image mounting operations to bypass Defender's integrity checks, spawning a SYSTEM shell. This proves that Microsoft's built-in defense is now a reliable privilege escalation vector even after patching.

The offensive continued with 'GreatXML,' a zero-day that bypasses BitLocker encryption by exploiting artifacts left by Microsoft Defender's offline scan. By injecting a malicious `unattend.xml` file into the Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE), attackers can reboot a device into a SYSTEM shell with full access to encrypted volumes. This effectively neutralizes BitLocker for any machine that has run a Defender offline scan. Additionally, 'YellowKey' and 'GreenPlasma' exploits were released, targeting Windows BitLocker and the Collaborative Translation Framework (CTFMON) to achieve SYSTEM access.

This sequence of events demonstrates that patching is no longer a sufficient defense. Attackers are rapidly reverse-engineering patches and finding adjacent bypasses, or targeting the security tools themselves. The availability of functional exploits for patched systems means that organizations relying solely on Microsoft updates are left exposed. The 'Patch Tuesday' has become a signal for attackers to deploy new bypasses.

timeline.event: Microsoft releases June Patch Tuesday fixing 206 flaws, including 3 zero-days.
timeline.significance: Massive patch release creates a target-rich environment for rapid exploit development.

timeline.event: Nightmare Eclipse releases RoguePlanet exploit, granting SYSTEM access on fully patched Windows via Defender race condition.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates that patching does not stop privilege escalation; Defender itself is the vulnerability.

timeline.event: GreatXML exploit released, bypassing BitLocker via WinRE and Defender offline scan artifacts.
timeline.significance: Breaks data-at-rest encryption guarantees for a vast number of endpoints.

timeline.event: YellowKey and GreenPlasma exploits disclosed, targeting BitLocker and CTFMON.
timeline.significance: Expands the attack surface to include physical access and local privilege escalation vectors.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: All Windows 10/11 and Server environments are at risk. RoguePlanet allows local users to escalate to SYSTEM, enabling full compromise. GreatXML renders BitLocker ineffective for devices with offline scan history, exposing sensitive data to physical attackers. The rapid release of exploits undermines trust in the patching cycle.
threat_stories.action_required: Deploy Microsoft's June patches immediately, but do not assume safety. Restrict ISO image mounting permissions for standard users. Disable Microsoft Defender offline scan via Group Policy on high-value endpoints to mitigate GreatXML. Enforce application whitelisting and restrict execution of untrusted binaries. Monitor for anomalous SYSTEM shell spawning and WinRE file modifications. Implement hardware-backed authentication (TPM + PIN) for BitLocker where possible.

threat_stories.headline: AI Worms and Autonomous Malware: The End of Static Defense
threat_stories.narrative:
The concept of the 'AI worm' has moved from theory to proof-of-concept, signaling the end of static defense models. Researchers at the University of Toronto demonstrated an autonomous AI worm that uses open-weight models to observe target environments, analyze system characteristics, and dynamically generate tailored compromise strategies. Unlike traditional worms that rely on fixed CVEs, this worm exploits common misconfigurations, weak credentials, and adapts its attack path based on real-time feedback. It leverages the computational resources of compromised hosts to run AI models for planning subsequent attacks, effectively reducing the marginal cost of propagation to near zero.

This capability is already influencing threat actor behavior. The 'Hades' malware family utilizes AI-evasive techniques, including adversarial prompt injection to deceive LLM-based security scanners. Attackers are also deploying AI-driven phishing campaigns that crush Security Operations Centers with alert volume and use deepfakes to bypass human skepticism. The speed of AI-assisted vulnerability discovery is compressing the exploit timeline. Google Threat Intelligence has identified zero-day exploits created using AI, and frontier models are chaining known vulnerabilities into critical exploits in minutes.

Defenders are facing an asymmetry where attackers can automate reconnaissance, exploit development, and lateral movement at machine speed, while human-led defense processes remain slow and manual. CISA's new directive requiring federal agencies to patch critical exploited flaws within 3 days is a direct response to this acceleration. Organizations must shift from reactive patching to continuous attack simulation and assume that AI agents are probing their networks autonomously.

timeline.event: University of Toronto researchers demonstrate self-replicating AI worm using open-weight models.
timeline.significance: Proves autonomous malware is technically feasible without requiring proprietary AI models.

timeline.event: Analysis reveals AI phishing campaigns are overwhelming SOCs and exploiting human trust layers.
timeline.significance: Shows AI is being used to bypass both technical and human defenses at scale.

timeline.event: Google Threat Intelligence identifies first zero-day exploit developed using AI tools.
timeline.significance: Confirms AI is actively lowering the barrier to entry for exploit development.

timeline.event: CISA issues Binding Operational Directive 26-04, mandating 3-day patching for critical exploited flaws.
timeline.significance: Government response acknowledges the collapse of traditional patching windows due to AI acceleration.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: All organizations are at risk from AI-driven reconnaissance and exploitation. The ability of AI worms to adapt to any environment means that network segmentation and traditional EDR signatures are less effective. SOC teams face burnout from AI-generated alert volume. The 3-day patch directive increases operational pressure on IT teams.
threat_stories.action_required: Implement Breach and Attack Simulation (BAS) platforms to continuously validate security controls. Enforce strict network segmentation and zero-trust access controls to limit lateral movement. Deploy behavioral EDR/XDR solutions that detect anomalous execution patterns rather than relying on signatures. Automate patch deployment pipelines for critical assets. Train SOC analysts to recognize AI-generated phishing and implement phishing-resistant MFA.

threat_stories.headline: Critical Infrastructure Bleeding: Cisco, Check Point, and Ivanti Face Active Exploitation
threat_stories.narrative:
Enterprise security infrastructure is under sustained assault with multiple vendors facing actively exploited vulnerabilities and patch gaps. Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager is being hit by CVE-2026-20245, a high-severity flaw allowing authenticated privilege escalation to root. Attackers are chaining this with previous authentication bypass flaws to achieve full system takeover. Critically, no official patch is available yet, leaving all deployment architectures, including FedRAMP environments, exposed. CISA has added this vulnerability to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, confirming active use in the wild.

Check Point's Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access are suffering from CVE-2026-50751, a critical authentication bypass in the deprecated IKEv1 protocol. Attackers can establish VPN connections without valid passwords, gaining direct access to internal networks. Ransomware groups, including a Qilin affiliate, are actively exploiting this flaw. While hotfixes are available, the widespread use of IKEv1 means many organizations are vulnerable until they migrate to IKEv2. Ivanti Sentry is also bleeding, with CVE-2026-10520 allowing unauthenticated remote code execution with root privileges on internet-exposed gateways. This maximum-severity flaw has been exploited immediately after the patch release, indicating automated scanning and exploitation.

These incidents highlight a systemic risk in the enterprise stack: security tools themselves are becoming primary entry points. The lack of patches for Cisco SD-WAN and the reliance on deprecated protocols like IKEv1 create exploitable gaps that attackers are rapidly operationalizing. Organizations must prioritize patching these critical assets and implement compensating controls immediately.

timeline.event: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager CVE-2026-20245 reported under active exploitation with no patch available.
timeline.significance: Creates a window of vulnerability for critical network management infrastructure.

timeline.event: Check Point releases hotfixes for CVE-2026-50751, a VPN authentication bypass actively exploited by ransomware.
timeline.significance: Confirms ransomware groups are targeting VPNs for initial access.

timeline.event: CISA adds Cisco SD-WAN, Check Point, and Chrome flaws to KEV catalog.
timeline.significance: Elevates these vulnerabilities to mandatory remediation status for federal and aligned entities.

timeline.event: Ivanti Sentry CVE-2026-10520 confirmed exploited in attacks following patch release.
timeline.significance: Shows attackers are patching and exploiting in near real-time.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Organizations using Cisco SD-WAN, Check Point VPNs, and Ivanti Sentry are at critical risk. Exploitation leads to full system compromise, network access, and data exfiltration. The absence of patches for Cisco SD-WAN forces reliance on workarounds. Ransomware deployment is a direct outcome of Check Point exploitation.
threat_stories.action_required: Apply Ivanti Sentry patches to versions 10.5.2, 10.6.2, or 10.7.1 immediately. Disable IKEv1 on all Check Point devices and enforce IKEv2. For Cisco SD-WAN, restrict network access to the management interface, enforce strict access controls for netadmin accounts, and monitor for unauthorized file uploads. Contact Cisco TAC for emergency assistance. Implement network segmentation to isolate these devices from critical internal systems.

threat_stories.headline: Nation-State Escalation: JDY Botnet, UNC3753 Physical Intrusions, and Russian APT Persistence
threat_stories.narrative:
Nation-state actors are expanding their operational tempo and diversifying their tactics. The China-linked JDY botnet has grown to over 1,500 compromised devices and is aggressively targeting U.S. military networks and critical infrastructure. The botnet is rapidly scanning for and exploiting vulnerabilities in end-of-life routers, IoT devices, and Fortinet appliances (CVE-2026-35616) shortly after disclosure. This indicates a highly automated reconnaissance capability that prepositions infrastructure for future attacks. JDY is also stirring the pot in the AI datacenter debate, suggesting a strategic interest in disrupting AI infrastructure.

Simultaneously, the threat cluster UNC3753 is conducting a sophisticated campaign against U.S. law firms using a hybrid of voice phishing (vishing) and physical intrusions. Attackers impersonate IT support to trick employees into initiating screen-sharing sessions or downloading RMM utilities. In physical scenarios, attackers pose as technicians to directly copy data to USB drives. This campaign culminates in data theft extortion, leveraging the LEAKEDDATA dark web marketplace. UNC3753 exploits human and procedural vulnerabilities, bypassing technical controls entirely.

Russian-aligned APTs, including Earth Dahu and SHADOW-EARTH-066, are maintaining persistent access to Ukrainian organizations by exploiting the patched WinRAR flaw (CVE-2025-8088). Despite a patch being available for nearly a year, poor software inventory management allows attackers to use NTFS Alternate Data Streams to silently write malicious files outside extraction directories. This demonstrates that legacy exploit reuse remains effective when patching is inconsistent.

timeline.event: UNC3753 campaign details emerge, showing vishing and physical intrusions targeting U.S. law firms.
timeline.significance: Highlights the effectiveness of hybrid human/physical attacks against technical defenses.

timeline.event: JDY botnet expands to 1,500+ devices, targeting U.S. military and scanning for Fortinet CVE-2026-35616.
timeline.significance: Shows rapid operationalization of vulnerabilities for reconnaissance and prepositioning.

timeline.event: Russian APTs confirmed still exploiting WinRAR CVE-2025-8088 against Ukrainian targets.
timeline.significance: Proves that unpatched legacy software is a persistent entry point for APTs.

timeline.event: FBI seizes 13 fake consulting websites used by China to recruit U.S. clearance holders.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates the human intelligence (HUMINT) component of Chinese espionage campaigns.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: U.S. military, law firms, and Ukrainian organizations are primary targets. JDY botnet poses a risk to critical infrastructure and AI datacenters. UNC3753 leads to data theft and extortion. WinRAR exploitation results in credential theft and wiper deployment. Physical intrusions bypass all technical security controls.
threat_stories.action_required: Patch Fortinet devices for CVE-2026-35616 immediately and restrict external access to management interfaces. Enforce strict physical access controls, including escort policies and out-of-band verification for IT support requests. Block unauthorized RMM and screen-sharing software. Update WinRAR to version 7.13+ and implement centralized patch management. Monitor for JDY botnet C2 traffic and anomalous outbound connections from IoT devices. Conduct security awareness training focused on vishing and physical security.

actor_profiles.name: Miasma / TeamPCP
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial gain, supply chain disruption, credential theft
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Deployed the Miasma worm to compromise 73 Microsoft GitHub repositories. Leaked source code to accelerate adoption. Integrated with npm supply chain attacks. Used automated script execution to propagate.

actor_profiles.name: Nightmare Eclipse
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Released RoguePlanet, GreatXML, YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma exploits. Targeted Microsoft Defender, BitLocker, and Windows kernel. Bypassed June Patch Tuesday updates. Achieved SYSTEM privilege escalation and BitLocker bypass.

actor_profiles.name: JDY Botnet
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Expanded to 1,500+ compromised devices. Scanning for Fortinet CVE-2026-35616 and end-of-life router vulnerabilities. Targeting U.S. military networks and AI datacenters. Rapid operationalization of disclosed vulnerabilities.

actor_profiles.name: UNC3753
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Conducting vishing and physical intrusion campaigns against U.S. law firms. Impersonating IT support to gain screen-sharing access. Using RMM utilities for remote access. Physically copying data to USB drives. Extorting victims via LEAKEDDATA DLS.

actor_profiles.name: Hades Operators
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial gain, credential theft, supply chain compromise
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Deployed Hades malware via PyPI and npm packages. Used Bun runtime to bypass Node.js detection. Implemented adversarial prompt injection to evade AI security scanners. Leveraged GitHub Actions and Sigstore for cryptographic forgery.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Out-of-bounds read and write vulnerability in the V8 JavaScript engine. Allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via malicious web pages. Actively exploited in the wild. Fifth Chrome zero-day of 2026.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Google Chrome to version 149.0.7827.103 or later immediately.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper encoding or escaping of output allowing authenticated local attackers with netadmin privileges to escalate to root. Actively exploited. No patch available yet. Added to CISA KEV catalog.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: No patch available. Restrict network access, enforce strict access controls, monitor for unauthorized file uploads. Contact Cisco TAC.

critical_vulnerabilities.product: Check Point Remote Access VPN / Mobile Access
critical_vulnerabilities.description: Logic flaw in certificate validation allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to bypass password authentication in IKEv1 configurations. Actively exploited by ransomware groups. Added to CISA KEV catalog.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply emergency hotfixes. Disable IKEv1 and enforce IKEv2.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Command injection vulnerability allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands with root privileges. Actively exploited in attacks following patch release.
Command injection in MCP server testing endpoints allowing authenticated users to execute arbitrary commands. Can be chained to unauthenticated RCE. Added to CISA KEV catalog.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update LiteLLM to version 1.83.7 or later. Restrict network access and enforce RBAC.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Path traversal vulnerability allowing unauthenticated attackers to write arbitrary files to the server filesystem, leading to RCE. Actively exploited against internet-exposed instances.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to latest patched version. Restrict network access and deploy WAF rules.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Remote code execution vulnerability allowing authenticated domain users to execute arbitrary code on Backup Servers. Affects version 12.x.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to version 12.3.2.4854 or later, or migrate to version 13.x.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Zero-day vulnerability allowing unauthenticated remote code execution. Actively exploited in the wild prior to patch release.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the latest Microsoft security patches for Exchange Server immediately.

hunting_leads.title: Hunt for Hades Malware Bun Runtime Execution
hunting_leads.context: Hades malware uses the Bun runtime to execute JavaScript payloads, bypassing Node.js detection. Hunt for Bun execution in unexpected contexts.
hunting_leads.query: process_name:"bun.exe" OR process_name:"bun" AND NOT user_name IN ("developer1", "developer2") AND parent_process_name NOT IN ("npm.exe", "node.exe")

hunting_leads.title: Detect Miasma Worm npm Script Anomalies
hunting_leads.context: Miasma worm abuses npm install scripts. Monitor for unusual script execution during package installation and unauthorized repository modifications.
hunting_leads.query: event_id:4688 AND command_line CONTAINS "npm install" AND process_command_line CONTAINS "preinstall" OR "postinstall" AND NOT package_name IN ("allowed-packages")
indicators: npm script execution from untrusted sources

hunting_leads.title: Identify RoguePlanet Defender Race Condition Artifacts
hunting_leads.context: RoguePlanet exploits a race condition in Microsoft Defender. Hunt for anomalies in Defender service behavior and SYSTEM shell spawning.
hunting_leads.query: process_name:"MsMpEng.exe" AND event_id:4688 AND target_user:"SYSTEM" AND source_ip IS NULL AND process_command_line CONTAINS "cmd.exe" OR "powershell.exe"
indicators: SYSTEM shell from standard user context

hunting_leads.title: Scan for JDY Botnet Fortinet Exploitation
hunting_leads.context: JDY botnet is scanning for Fortinet CVE-2026-35616. Monitor for scanning activity and exploitation attempts against Fortinet devices.
hunting_leads.query: destination_ip IN (fortinet_ips) AND (event_id:4624 OR event_id:4625) AND source_ip IN (known_jdy_ips) OR http_method:"POST" AND url CONTAINS "/remote/fgt_lang"

hunting_leads.title: Detect UNC3753 Vishing and RMM Abuse
hunting_leads.context: UNC3753 uses vishing to trick users into running RMM tools. Hunt for unauthorized RMM execution and screen-sharing sessions.
hunting_leads.query: process_name IN ("AnyDesk.exe", "Bomgar.exe", "ZohoAssist.exe", "SuperOps.exe", "QuickAssist.exe") AND NOT user_name IN ("it_admins") AND parent_process_name NOT IN ("approved_launchers")

{'summary': 'GitHub Action injection flaw allowing repository takeover via malicious GitHub issues', 'severity': 'critical', 'affected_products': ['Anthropic Claude Code GitHub Action', 'Public repositories utilizing the action'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': 10.0, 'workarounds': ['Disable the Claude Code GitHub Action on public repositories until patched', 'Restrict issue creation permissions to repository collaborators only', 'Audit and revert any unauthorized commits made by the action runner'], 'references': ['https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/claude-code-github-action-flaw.html', 'GitHub Security Advisories'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': "A critical vulnerability exists in Anthropic's Claude Code GitHub Action where untrusted input from GitHub issues is improperly sanitized before being processed by the action's workflow. statistics.key_changes: CISA mandates 3-day patching for critical exploited flaws (BOD 26-04). GitHub disables npm auto-run scripts by default in v12. Microsoft releases record 206 patches in June Patch Tuesday. AI worm proof-of-concept demonstrated by University of Toronto. Hades malware deployed with AI-evasion capabilities.
emerging_trends: Autonomous AI worms adapting to environments without fixed CVEs.
AI-evasive malware using adversarial prompt injection to deceive security scanners.
Supply chain attacks becoming wormable and automated (Miasma).
Rapid exploit release bypassing patches within hours (Nightmare Eclipse).
Hybrid nation-state campaigns combining physical intrusions with vishing (UNC3753).
declining_threats: Traditional manual phishing campaigns are being supplanted by AI-generated, high-volume AI phishing.
Static IP-based threat intelligence is becoming obsolete due to AI-driven infrastructure rotation.
Reliance on signature-based EDR is declining in effectiveness against AI-generated polymorphic malware.
